title: Programs of the POEA to Protect the Public from Illegal Recruitment
content: The POEA shall adopt policies and procedures, prepare and implement programs toward the eradication of illegal recruitment activities including: 1) Providing legal assistance to victims of illegal recruitment and related cases; 2) Assistance in the prosecution of suspected illegal recruiters; 3) Special operations such as surveillance of persons and entities suspected to be engaged in illegal recruitment; and 4) Information and education campaigns. The Administration shall coordinate with other appropriate entities as necessary. The POEA provides free legal assistance to victims including legal advice, preparation of complaints, institution of criminal actions, and counseling during investigations and hearings. If ongoing illegal recruitment is confirmed through surveillance, the Administration shall issue closure orders to close illegal recruitment establishments/entities.

metadata.source_file: 2002 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of OFWs Primer.pdf
